MIAMI: March 2017.
after Donald Trump signed latest executive order roll back Clean Power favor fossil fuels, Airports Council International-North America (ACI-NA) awarded Miami International Airport (MIA) 2017 award environmental management.
acknowledged largest energy-saving programs eastern U.S.
Launched August 2015 with local utility Florida Power Light, project saving million gallons water year, reducing carbon emissions equal 5,110 automobiles annually, cutting airport's utility bill US$40 million over years.
addition being America's busiest airport international freight, international passenger gateways, leading economic engine, honored recognized leader environmental management well," said Miami-Dade Aviation director Emilio González.
"Being energy-smart airport also smart business.
US$40 million savings helps decrease operating expenses passed current prospective airlines, which turn spurs investment local economy," added.
Other ACI-NA winners 2017 were Indianapolis Airport Authority (Outreach, Education Community Involvement Award); Minneapolis-St. Paul Metropolitan Airports Commission (Special/Innovative Projects Award); Billy Bishop Toronto City Airport (Mitigation Award); Detroit Metropolitan Airport runner Environmental Management Award.
Oklahoma attorney general, Scott Pruitt sued agency heads block Clean Power from taking effect, calling "executive fiat" that violates states' rights.
Pictured: Miami-Dade Aviation Department assistant director Mark Hatfield (center) receives ACI-NA Environmental Achievement Award Environmental Management from ACI-NA Environmental Affairs Committee chairman Hough (left) ACI-NA Board chairman William Vanecek.

Epidemiol Community Health.
2006 Apr;60(4):364-72.
What does self rated health measure?
Results from British Whitehall French Gazel cohort studies.
Singh-Manoux Martikainen Ferrie Zins Marmot Goldberg
investigate determinants self rated health (SRH) women British Whitehall study French Gazel cohort study.
cross sectional analyses reported this paper data from wave Whitehall study (1985-88) wave Gazel study (1990).
Determinants were either self reported obtained through medical screening employer's records.
Whitehall study based civil service departments located London.
Gazel study based employees France's national electricity company (EDF-GDF).
data were available 6889 3403 women Whitehall 4688 women Gazel.
Correlation analysis used identify determinants from measures Whitehall Gazel.
Stepwise multiple regressions identified five determinants (symptom score, sickness absence, longstanding illness, minor psychiatric morbidity, number recurring health problems) Whitehall explaining 34.7% variance SRH.
Gazel, four measures (physical tiredness, number health problems past year, physical mobility, number prescription drugs used) explained 41.4% variance SRH.
Measures mental physical health status contribute most construct.
part played age, early life factors, family history, sociodemographic variables, psychosocial factors, health behaviours these occupational cohorts modest.
